commands.review_summary:
Adds an exact argv-sensitive `agents add` startup policy that skips plugin preload for no-bind config-only add invocations and adds focused startup/pre-action regression tests.

PR surface: Source +38, Tests +268. Total +306 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main resolves startup policy before `agentsAddCommand` and inherits the parent `agents` plugin preload policy for `agents add`; I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer acceptance of a compatibility-sensitive startup behavior change, not a narrow automated repair.

target.title: fix(cli): skip plugin preload for config-only agents add
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #66936.

- Add an exact startup policy for `agents add` that skips plugin preload only when argv selects the documented config-only/non-interactive add path.
- Keep interactive `agents add` on the existing all-plugin preload path so the wizard can still see plugin-backed setup/auth surfaces.
- Keep `agents add --bind ...` on the plugin-aware path because binding account scope can depend on loaded channel plugin metadata.
- Add focused startup-policy and pre-action regression tests covering separated/equal flag forms, `--json` alone, and flags after `--`.

## AI Assistance Disclosure

Prepared with AI assistance. I reviewed and understand the affected path from CLI pre-action startup policy through `agents add` command registration and the config-only add command body.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `openclaw agents add <id> --workspace <dir> --non-interactive --json` no longer preloads plugins before the config-only flow runs.

Real environment tested: Built CLI via `node dist/entry.js` on Linux with isolated `HOME`, `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`; no real credentials or user OpenClaw state were used.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
pnpm build

timeout 4s env \
  HOME="$HOME_DIR" \
  OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR="$STATE_DIR" \
  OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$CONFIG_PATH" \
  HOLD_OPEN_MARKER="$CONTROL_MARKER" \
  OPENCLAW_HIDE_BANNER=1 \
  node dist/entry.js agents add interactive-smoke

timeout 4s env \
  HOME="$HOME_DIR" \
  OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR="$STATE_DIR" \
  OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$CONFIG_PATH" \
  HOLD_OPEN_MARKER="$BIND_MARKER" \
  OPENCLAW_HIDE_BANNER=1 \
  node dist/entry.js agents add bind-smoke --workspace "$WORKSPACE_DIR-bind" --bind hold-open --json

timeout 8s env \
  HOME="$HOME_DIR" \
  OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR="$STATE_DIR" \
  OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$CONFIG_PATH" \
  HOLD_OPEN_MARKER="$FIX_MARKER" \
  OPENCLAW_HIDE_BANNER=1 \
  node dist/entry.js agents add smoke --workspace "$WORKSPACE_DIR" --non-interactive --json
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
control_status=124
control_marker_exists=yes
bind_status=124
bind_marker_exists=yes
fixed_status=0
fixed_marker_exists=no
fixed_stdout_first_lines=
{
  "agentId": "smoke",
  "name": "smoke",
  "workspace": "$RUN_DIR/workspaces/smoke",
  "agentDir": "$RUN_DIR/state/agents/smoke/agent",
  "bindings": {
    "added": [],
    "updated": [],
    "skipped": [],
    "conflicts": []
  }
}
proof_passed=1
```

Observed result after fix: The control command proves plugin preload is observable and can hold the process open. The `--bind` command also preloads the proof plugin, preserving plugin-aware binding behavior. The no-bind config-only `agents add` command exits 0, writes the expected JSON payload, and does not import the proof plugin.

What was not tested: No live provider credentials or real plugin accounts were exercised; the proof uses isolated local state and a synthetic plugin-preload marker. GitHub PR checks cover the remote CI gate for this PR.

## Root Cause

`registerPreActionHooks` resolves startup policy before the `agents add` action computes whether the command is interactive or config-only. The command catalog had a broad parent `agents` policy with `loadPlugins: "always"`, and no exact `agents add` override. As a result, config-only invocations inherited plugin preload before reaching `agentsAddCommand`, so a plugin import/auth problem could hang a command that only needed to update config.

## Dependency contract

No external dependency behavior is needed for this fix. The relevant source contracts are internal:

- `src/cli/program/register.agent.ts` selects the non-interactive/config-only `agents add` path when explicit add flags are present: `workspace`, `model`, `agentDir`, `bind`, or `nonInteractive`.
- `src/cli/argv.ts` helpers support both `--flag value` and `--flag=value`, and stop scanning at `--`.
- `src/cli/command-bootstrap.ts` returns before plugin registry loading when startup policy resolves `loadPlugins: false`.
- `src/commands/agents.bindings.ts` can resolve omitted binding account IDs from loaded channel plugins, so `--bind` intentionally remains plugin-aware.

## Regression Test Plan

- `src/cli/command-path-policy.test.ts`: verifies the exact `agents add` policy merge and preserves the parent network-proxy bypass.
- `src/cli/command-startup-policy.test.ts`: verifies interactive add, `--json` alone, and `--bind` still load plugins; no-bind config-only add flags skip plugins; flags after `--` are ignored.
- `src/cli/command-execution-startup.test.ts`: verifies startup context distinguishes interactive and config-only add.
- `src/cli/program/preaction.test.ts`: verifies pre-action skips plugin registry loading for no-bind config-only add but still loads plugins for interactive and `--bind` add.

## Security Impact

This reduces accidental plugin code execution for a config-only CLI path. It does not change plugin trust policy, plugin registry behavior, credentials, SecretRef resolution, or provider auth handling.

## Human Verification

I checked the issue context, exact open/closed PR searches for `66936`, prior merged PR #74220, and the current command source before patching. No open PR currently addresses this residual `agents add` startup path.

## CODEOWNERS / owner review

No direct `.github/CODEOWNERS` rule matches the touched `src/cli/**` files. Normal CLI/startup-policy maintainer review is expected.

## Changelog

No `CHANGELOG.md` edit. This is contributor PR release-note material only.

## Review conversations

No GitHub review conversations exist for this PR yet. Local review checks:

- `autoreview`: clean, no accepted/actionable findings.
- `codex review --base origin/main`: no actionable correctness issues found.
- CI follow-up: `check-additional-boundaries-bcd` first failed on stale-base QQ bot raw fetch, then on current-main MiniMax raw fetch. Both were resolved by rebasing past upstream fixes; no non-CLI code remains in this branch.

## Risks

- The startup policy intentionally mirrors the explicit config-only flag list from `register.agent.ts`, except `--bind` stays plugin-aware to preserve binding account resolution. Future add flags that select the config-only path must update both places.
- The fix is argv-sensitive by design, so tests cover `--flag value`, `--flag=value`, and `--` terminator behavior.

## Behavior tradeoffs

- `--json` alone is not treated as config-only and still follows the interactive add preload path.
- Interactive `agents add` still preloads plugins so plugin-backed setup/auth surfaces remain available to the wizard.
- `agents add --bind ...` still preloads plugins; the no-preload shortcut applies only to config-only add calls without binding specs.

## Scope boundaries

- Does not change `agents list`, SecretRef behavior, Telegram status paths, provider status rendering, config schema, or plugin loader internals.
- Does not add compatibility shims or broad plugin lifecycle changes.

## Validation

```sh
pnpm docs:list
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/command-startup-policy.test.ts src/cli/command-path-policy.test.ts src/cli/command-execution-startup.test.ts src/cli/program/preaction.test.ts
pnpm run lint:tmp:no-raw-channel-fetch
OPENCLAW_ADDITIONAL_BOUNDARY_SHARD='2/4,3/4,4/4' OPENCLAW_ADDITIONAL_BOUNDARY_CONCURRENCY=4 OPENCLAW_EXTENSION_BOUNDARY_CONCURRENCY=6 node scripts/run-additional-boundary-checks.mjs
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/cli/command-catalog.ts src/cli/command-path-policy.test.ts src/cli/command-startup-policy.test.ts src/cli/command-execution-startup.test.ts src/cli/program/preaction.test.ts
git diff --check
pnpm build
# real CLI proof shown above
```

Results: focused Vitest passed 4 files / 50 tests, no-raw-channel-fetch passed, the full failed additional-boundary shard passed after rebasing past upstream fixes, format check passed, `git diff --check` passed, build passed, and the isolated real CLI proof passed.
